chapter_context.summary: Envisions the mountain of the Lord's house established in the latter days as a beacon of peace for all nations, followed by warnings of the Day of the Lord against human pride.

outline.text: The Vision of the Mountain of the Lord's House in the Latter Days
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Vision of the Mountain of the Lord's House in the Latter Days (2:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Confronting Pride, Idolatry, and Wealth in the Land
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Confronting Pride, Idolatry, and Wealth in the Land (2:5-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Day of the Lord Against All High and Haughty Human Works
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Day of the Lord Against All High and Haughty Human Works (2:12-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 2, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
